evals.query: Write a black-box end-to-end test for my Kora service that runs the packaged Docker image and checks POST /users returns 201 and GET /users/999999 returns 404.
expected_behavior: Defines an AppContainer extending GenericContainer<AppContainer> built from the application Dockerfile via ImageFromDockerfile
Exposes ports 8080 and 8085 and gates startup with Wait.forHttp("/system/readiness").forPort(8085).forStatusCode(200)
Annotates the test class with @Testcontainers and the container fields with @Container
Drives the application through java.net.http.HttpClient against the public port URI and asserts status codes 201 and 404
Does not use @KoraAppTest, @TestComponent, field injection, or in-process graph injection for the black-box test

evals.query: My Kora app needs a real PostgreSQL for its black-box test. How do I start Postgres with Testcontainers and point the app at it?
expected_behavior: Declares a PostgreSQLContainer on Network.SHARED with withNetworkAliases("postgres")
Uses AppContainer.dependsOn(POSTGRES) so the database starts first
Injects connection settings with withEnv such as POSTGRES_JDBC_URL = jdbc:postgresql://postgres:5432/<db>, POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASS
Notes that the env var names must match the application's ${VAR} substitution keys in its @ConfigSource config
Does not create a fresh Network.newNetwork() per test and does not use field injection

evals.query: Set up the Gradle build and Dockerfile so my black-box module can package and test the Kora application.
expected_behavior: Pins Kora artifacts through the ru.tinkoff.kora:kora-parent BOM and includes the annotation processor (annotationProcessor "ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors", or ksp "ru.tinkoff.kora:symbol-processors" for Kotlin)
Adds testImplementation for ru.tinkoff.kora:test-junit5 and org.testcontainers:junit-jupiter / testcontainers / postgresql
Configures test { dependsOn ":app:distTar" } so the distribution archive is built before tests
Writes a Dockerfile based on eclipse-temurin JRE that unpacks build/distributions/application.tar and EXPOSEs 8080 and 8085
Does not version individual ru.tinkoff.kora:* dependencies and does not introduce a non-Kora application packaging plugin

evals.query: I prefer RestAssured for HTTP assertions. Show a black-box test for a Product API CRUD flow.
expected_behavior: Adds the io.rest-assured:rest-assured test dependency and sets RestAssured.baseURI from AppContainer.getURI()
Uses the given().when().then() DSL with statusCode() and body() matchers for create/read/update/delete
Reuses the AppContainer (GenericContainer subclass) waiting on /system/readiness on port 8085
Asserts realistic CRUD status codes (201 create, 200 read, 204 delete, 404 missing)
Does not use an in-process mock web layer or @KoraAppTest in place of the running container

evals.query: My Kora service consumes a Kafka topic and updates an order status. Write a black-box test that publishes an event and verifies processing via HTTP.
expected_behavior: Starts a KafkaContainer on Network.SHARED with a network alias and injects withEnv("KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS", "kafka:9092") into the AppContainer
Publishes a record with a plain org.apache.kafka.clients.producer.KafkaProducer using getBootstrapServers() for the host-mapped port
Uses Awaitility await().atMost(...).untilAsserted(...) to poll the HTTP API until the order status is PROCESSED
Verifies the side effect only through the public HTTP endpoint, not by reaching into the consumer
Does not modify the Kora graph or add test components into the running application

evals.query: Add black-box tests for error handling: invalid request body, missing resource, and duplicate email.
expected_behavior: Asserts 400 for an invalid/empty JSON body (exercising @Json deserialization and @Valid validation through the real boundary)
Asserts 404 for a GET on a non-existent id
Asserts 409 for creating a resource with a duplicate unique value
Sends requests through HttpClient or RestAssured against the running AppContainer
Does not assert by calling controller or service methods directly and only checks responses at the HTTP boundary

evals.query: In CI I build and push the Docker image separately. How do I make the black-box test use a pre-built image instead of building the Dockerfile each run?
expected_behavior: Branches the AppContainer construction on System.getenv("APP_IMAGE"): when set, builds from DockerImageName.parse(appImage); otherwise from ImageFromDockerfile with the application Dockerfile
Keeps the same exposed ports and Wait.forHttp("/system/readiness") readiness gating regardless of image source
Describes the CI flow: build image, push to registry, export APP_IMAGE, run ./gradlew test
Does not hardcode container ports (uses getMappedPort) and does not depend on a local JDK at test time when APP_IMAGE is set

evals.query: Should I write a black-box test or a component test to verify my UserService business logic in isolation?
expected_behavior: Recommends a component test with @KoraAppTest and @TestComponent (the kora-testing-junit skill) for isolated business logic, not a black-box test
Explains black-box tests run the packaged Docker image through HTTP and are best for end-to-end contract validation
Notes that black-box does not mock collaborators while @KoraAppTest supports @TestComponent mocks with Mockito/MockK and KoraConfigModification
Does not suggest mixing @KoraAppTest with GenericContainer in one test, and uses @TestComponent (not field injection) for mocks
